title: Close scope.bounded_contexts enum gap - schema + Validate both enforce closed set

evidence.ref: Audit found scope.bounded_contexts had no enum constraint in decisions/schema.riido.json and decision.Validate did not check the values either. scope.areas was already enumerated/enforced (11 values) — bounded_contexts was the asymmetric gap. Typos like platfrm/Platform/work_item would have silently entered the SSOT
031-forbid-ssot-schemas - same asymmetry-detection class: explicit invariants enforced symmetrically across SSOT halves

guards.ref: decisions/schema.riido.json - scope.bounded_contexts.items.enum set to [platform, work-item]; rejects unknown BCs at parse time

internal/decision.Validate (validBoundedContexts map mirrors the schema enum) - rejects unknown BCs at load time before any cross-check runs. ValidateMap-style symmetry with validAreas already in place

internal/decision/decision_test.go TestValidateAcceptsKnownBoundedContexts + TestValidateRejectsUnknownBoundedContexts (5 typo variants: NotARealBC, platfrm, empty string, Platform/Capitalized, work_item) + TestValidateRejectsMixedKnownAndUnknownBoundedContexts
concept-map C-020 - codifies the enum convention; concept-map bumped v0.23.0 to v0.24.0

examples: Existing decision with bounded_contexts: [platform] still validates - all 35 records on main use only platform or work-item.

New decision with bounded_contexts: [platfrm] (typo) is rejected by both layers: JSON Schema reports enum violation; decision.Validate reports 'scope.bounded_contexts: unknown bounded context "platfrm"'.
Adding a third BC requires a successor decision that updates BOTH the JSON Schema enum AND the validBoundedContexts map - same convention as ValidateMap and validAreas.
counterexamples: scope.areas remains untouched - it already had the enum+enforcement before D036.

The first test file in internal/decision/ — backlog audit also noted 7 untested internal/ packages; D036 establishes the test-file convention for decision/. Other packages remain TODO (separate decisions when warranted).
Case-sensitivity is enforced: 'Platform' is rejected, only 'platform' is accepted. No fuzzy-matching - keeps the enum small and deliberate.
